rationale: The citing paper uses Nunes and Park (2003) in its discussion to interpret a core empirical finding: consumers responded more to reward-point promotions than to equivalent price discounts. It specifically draws on the cited paper's idea that people may not readily convert one currency into another (e.g., points to money), which can alter valuation. Although the citation is not a modeling foundation and appears as interpretive support rather than a full theoretical backbone, it plays a clear conceptual role beyond a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Nunes and Park's incommensurate-currency insight to explain why item-based loyalty points can be more behaviorally effective than same-value monetary discounts. It transfers that idea into a loyalty-program field setting to interpret observed promotion responsiveness.
